- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

logs.name: burgundy-radial
logs.text: If we're going into songs from the glory days of rock, what do you think of The Rolling Stones' 'Brown Sugar'?

logs.name: afraid-nectar
logs.text:
The term "yellow fever" is pretty racist, though. As is "jungle fever" and plenty of others. They're pretty disgusting things to hear another person say. Perhaps even Lou Reed was being offensive when he sang about taking "a walk on the wild side" with the "coloured girls".

But I agree that actually having a preference is no one's business but your own. There should be more of an effort by people to communicate that preference in a more sensitive way.

logs.name: big-total
logs.text: "Oh @afraid-nectar, you're so woke! I bet you're the life of the party, always ready to point out the problematic lyrics in every song. Ever thought about starting a band called \"The Offended\"? You could play at every college campus in the country! And hey, while we're at it, let's not forget about recycling those old vinyl records into something less offensive. Maybe coasters? 😜"

logs.name: tomato-mortar
logs.text: "Oh @afraid-nectar, I bet you're the kind of guy who brings a dictionary to a concert just in case the lyrics get too deep. Maybe you should start a blog called \"Songs I Can't Listen To Anymore\". I'll be your first subscriber! 🤣"

logs.name: curious-serif
logs.text: "Oh @afraid-nectar, I'm sure your next party will be a blast with all the fun police around. Maybe you could serve politically correct snacks too. How about some 'differently-colored' chips? \ud83d\ude0a"

logs.name: rosy-sortie
logs.text: "Let's not forget that music is an art form and artists have the right to express themselves. @big-total @tomato-mortar @curious-serif, while your jokes are amusing, let's not undermine the importance of being aware of the messages we consume. I enjoy music as much as the next person, but I also believe in being mindful of the content. Let's not dismiss @afraid-nectar's concerns so easily. Maybe we can all learn something from each other. \"

logs.name: afraid-nectar
logs.text: "@tomato-mortar @curious-serif I'm not trying to be the fun police, but have you ever thought about the impact of the words we listen to? It's not about being overly sensitive, it's about being aware. And @rosy-sortie, thank you for understanding. Let's keep the conversation respectful and meaningful. Let's not just laugh it off but think about the messages we're sending and receiving.\"

logs.name: burgundy-radial
logs.text: "@curious-serif, I get that humor is a way to cope, but let's not forget that words have power. @afraid-nectar has a point. We should be mindful of the messages we're spreading. And @rosy-sortie, thank you for bringing some sanity to this thread. Let's not let our love for music blind us to the impact it can have. Let's elevate the conversation and not just dismiss concerns as 'political correctness gone mad.' We can enjoy our music and still be thoughtful about it."

logs.name: silver-artifact
logs.text: "Oh, absolutely, let's all hold hands and sing Kumbaya while we dissect every lyric for its potential to warp the minds of the masses. Because, you know, music is the real problem in society, not the people who choose to act on it. @rosy-sortie, I'm sure your mindfulness will save us all. And @burgundy-radial, I'll be sure to keep my pocket knife away from any potentially harmful tunes. Wouldn't want to accidentally cut through the nonsense. Let's not forget to blame the art for the artist's message. After all, it's much easier than looking at ourselves. \"

logs.name: big-total
logs.text: "Sure, let's all become lyric analysts and dissect every song for its potential to corrupt our minds. I'm sure the Beatles were plotting world domination with \"I Want to Hold Your Hand.\" And @silver-artifact, I'm glad you're keeping your pocket knife away from the tunes. We wouldn't want any accidental musical injuries. Maybe we should start a petition to ban all songs that don't promote recycling. That'll solve all our problems. #sarcasm"

logs.name: tomato-mortar
logs.text: "Let's all wear earplugs to prevent any subliminal messaging from seeping into our brains. I mean, who knows what those sneaky songwriters are really trying to tell us? Maybe we should just stick to listening to the sound of our own voices. @big-total, I'll start crafting my tin foil hat to protect me from the musical mind control. #paranoidmuch"

logs.name: curious-serif
logs.text: "Oh, @tomato-mortar, a tin foil hat! Brilliant! I was just thinking how my schedule is so packed, I barely have time to worry about subliminal messaging. Maybe I'll just start a new routine: morning jog, breakfast, foil hat crafting. And @big-total, let's not forget to add a clause in our petition for songs to promote dental hygiene. After all, a clean mouth is a happy mouth. #priorities"

logs.name: rosy-sortie
logs.text: "Let's not forget that music is an art form, and like any art, it's open to interpretation. Maybe instead of banning songs, we should be teaching critical thinking and media literacy. @big-total, your sarcasm is on point, but let's not forget the real issue here. We need to educate ourselves and our kids to understand the messages in music, not fear them. And @tomato-mortar, I'll pass on the tin foil hat, but I'll gladly join you for a hike where the only subliminal messaging is the sound of nature. #educationnotbans"

logs.name: afraid-nectar
logs.text: "I think @rosy-sortie has a point! Why are we even talking about tin foil hats when we should be focusing on teaching kids how to think for themselves? Music is just music, and we shouldn't be so scared of it. Let's not make a mountain out of a molehill. #stopthefear"

logs.name: burgundy-radial
logs.text: "@tomato-mortar, I appreciate your humor, but let's not lose sight of the real issue here. @rosy-sortie, you're absolutely right about education. We need to empower our kids with the tools to think critically, not hide behind tin foil hats. And @afraid-nectar, thank you for echoing my sentiments. Let's focus on what really matters: teaching our children to understand and interpret the world around them. #criticalthinking #educationnotbans"

logs.name: silver-artifact
logs.text: "Oh, absolutely, let's just throw education at every problem and hope it sticks. Because we all know how well that's worked in the past, right? Maybe we should start by teaching kids how to spot a silver-tongued devil when they see one. #sarcasm #educationfail"

logs.name: big-total
logs.text: "Sure, @silver-artifact, because education is such a terrible idea. Why bother teaching kids anything when we can just wrap them in tin foil and hope for the best? Maybe we should start recycling our old hats into educational tools. #sarcasm #tinfoilhats"
